Name: Rice Space Institute (RSI)
Relevance: The primary interdisciplinary hub at Rice for coordinating space research, education, and industry partnerships.

Name: Rice Eclipse
Relevance: Student-led team focused on the design, manufacturing, and testing of hybrid and liquid-propellant sounding rockets and engines.

Name: AIAA Rice (American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics)
Relevance: Student chapter supporting aerospace professional development and serving as the umbrella for flight-based competition teams.

Name: Rice Flight
Relevance: Competition team under AIAA Rice that designs and manufactures autonomous UAVs and Payload Autonomous Delivery Aircraft (PADA).

Name: SEDS Rice (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space)
Relevance: Student chapter focused on space advocacy, outreach, and multi-disciplinary space-related engineering projects.

Name: OwlSat
Relevance: A student-led CubeSat project selected by NASA's CubeSat Launch Initiative (CSLI) to study solar radiation and orbital decay.

Name: Center for Advanced Space Sensing Technologies (CASST)
Relevance: New US Space Force-backed center focused on developing breakthrough remote sensing technologies and AI-driven space situational awareness.

Name: Space Plasma Physics Group
Relevance: Research group examining near-Earth, solar, and planetary plasmas using computational modeling and space-based observatories.

Name: Department of Space Science (Historical)
Relevance: Established in 1963 as the first of its kind in the US; later merged into the Department of Physics and Astronomy.

Name: Kavraki Lab
Relevance: Conducts research in motion planning and human-robot interaction with direct applications to NASA’s Robonaut 2 program.

Name: MAHI Lab (Mechatronics and Haptic Interfaces)
Relevance: Develops haptic systems and wearable garments for astronaut training and robotic teleoperation in space environments.

Name: EEPS Planetary Science Group
Relevance: Research division within the Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences department focusing on planetary formation and evolution.

Name: Toffoletto Research Group
Relevance: Focuses on large-scale numerical modeling of the Earth's magnetosphere and plasma transport within the inner magnetosphere.

Name: Alexander Lab
Relevance: Led by the RSI Director, focusing on solar physics, coronal dynamics, and the development of space instrumentation.

Name: Isella Lab
Relevance: Utilizes radio interferometry (ALMA) to study planet formation, protoplanetary disks, and star-forming regions.

Name: Johns-Krull Lab
Relevance: Conducts observational research on young stars, exoplanets, and stellar magnetic activity using high-resolution spectroscopy.

Name: Liang Group
Relevance: Investigates theoretical high-energy astrophysics, including black hole accretion disks, gamma-ray bursts, and plasma physics.

Name: RiSYS Lab (Ghorbel)
Relevance: Researches dynamic systems and control for robotics with applications in autonomous flight and industrial inspection.

Name: Cohan Research Group
Relevance: Utilizes satellite data and atmospheric modeling to study air quality and the impact of climate on Earth systems.

Name: Killian Lab
Relevance: Specializes in ultracold neutral plasmas, providing fundamental insights into plasma dynamics relevant to astrophysical environments.

Name: RUNSRA (Rice National Security Research Accelerator)
Relevance: Coordinates research efforts with the DoD and Space Force, specifically in aerospace and national security technology.

Name: Apollo ALSEP Instrument Teams (Historical)
Relevance: Historical teams that designed and managed the Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package (ALSEP) instruments deployed on the moon.

Name: Rice Robotics Club
Relevance: Broad student group that frequently develops UAVs and robotic systems for intercollegiate competitions and technical projects.

Name: Rice Electric Vehicle (REV) Team
Relevance: Focuses on high-efficiency power systems and electric propulsion architectures applicable to future aerospace vehicles.

Name: Nittrouer Lab
Relevance: Employs remote sensing technologies to study geomorphic processes on Earth and other planetary bodies.
